message:
perf(combinator): replace RwLock metrics with atomic counters on circuit breaker and rate limiter hot paths

The circuit breaker and rate limiter previously acquired a write lock on
every success/failure/rejection to increment metrics counters. Under high
concurrency this creates needless contention because the RwLock serializes
all writers even when the operation is a simple counter increment.

CircuitBreaker:
- Add AtomicU64 fields for total_success, total_failure, total_rejected,
  total_ignored_errors and AtomicU32 for current_failure_streak
- Hot paths (record_success, record_failure, acquire rejection) now use
  fetch_add/store on atomics instead of acquiring the metrics write lock
- The RwLock is still used for rare state-change events that need to update
  multiple fields atomically (times_opened, times_closed, current_state)
- metrics() reads atomics and merges with locked rare-event counters
- Add optimistic fast path for record_success in Closed{failures:0} state
  (common happy path) that skips the CAS loop entirely
- Extract update_state_metrics and populate_metrics_snapshot helpers to
  reduce duplication in state transition callback paths

RateLimiter + SlidingWindowRateLimiter:
- Add AtomicU64 fields for total_allowed, total_rejected, total_waited
- try_acquire, execute, enqueue, and process_queue hot paths now increment
  atomics instead of taking the write lock
- process_queue avg_wait_time calculation now uses atomic total_waited with
  a zero-division guard instead of the locked field
- metrics() reads atomics and merges with locked wait-time stats
- Remove stale lock-ordering comments that no longer apply

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
